message:
fix(mesh): add explicit u64 type suffix to XorFilter bit-shifts and guard IBLT against zero max_changes

XorFilterPlaceholder: The bit-shift operations `1 << bit_idx` were using
the default integer type (i32), which would silently overflow when bit_idx
>= 32 on targets where the compiler infers i32. Changed to `1u64 << bit_idx`
in both insert() and maybe_contains() to match the u64 element type of the
bits vector and prevent undefined-behavior-adjacent shifts.

IbltPlaceholder::note_local_change: When max_changes is configured to 0,
the previous code would enter an impossible state — the `if` guard
(len >= max_changes) would be true but pop_front on an empty deque is a
no-op, creating an infinite loop. Now:
- Early-return when max_changes == 0 (still increments change_seq for
  monotonic sequence tracking)
- Changed `if` to `while` for the eviction loop to correctly drain excess
  entries when the deque somehow exceeds capacity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
